name: Kotlin
description: A modern, concise JVM language — the preferred language for Android development and a joy to write.

lessons.title: Hello World & Basics
lessons.content: Kotlin is concise and expressive. The main function is the entry point. println() prints output. Kotlin infers types, so you rarely need to write them explicitly. Semicolons are optional.

exercises.question: How are functions declared in Kotlin?

lessons.title: val vs var & Null Safety
lessons.content: Kotlin uses 'val' for immutable (read-only) variables and 'var' for mutable. Kotlin is null-safe by default — types cannot be null unless explicitly marked with ?. The ?: operator provides a default for nulls.
lessons.code_example:
val name: String = "Alice"   // immutable
var age: Int = 25             // mutable
age = 26                      // OK

var city: String? = null      // nullable
val display = city ?: "Unknown"
println(display) // Unknown

exercises.question: Which Kotlin keyword declares an immutable variable?
What does String? mean in Kotlin?
hints: The ? marks a type as able to hold null

lessons.title: Data Classes
lessons.content: Data classes in Kotlin automatically generate equals(), hashCode(), toString(), and copy(). They are perfect for holding data. Declare them with the 'data class' keyword.
lessons.code_example:
data class User(
    val id: Int,
    val name: String,
    val email: String
)

val user1 = User(1, "Alice", "alice@email.com")
val user2 = user1.copy(name = "Bob")

println(user1) // User(id=1, name=Alice, ...)
println(user2) // User(id=1, name=Bob, ...)

exercises.question: What does data class automatically generate?
options: Nothing — you still write everything manually
hints: Data classes are a convenience feature that generates boilerplate

lessons.title: Extension Functions & Lambdas
lessons.content: Extension functions add methods to existing classes without modifying them. Lambdas are anonymous functions. Together they make Kotlin code highly expressive and concise.

exercises.question: What do extension functions allow you to do?
options: Add new methods to classes without modifying them
exercises.answer: Add new methods to classes without modifying them
hints: fun String.myMethod() — extends String without changing its source
